assertion_results.text: The answer recommends using pytest fixtures for the shared setup
assertion_results.evidence: "What mechanism to use: Use pytest fixtures (with yield for setup/teardown) and appropriate scoping."

assertion_results.text: The answer mentions `conftest.py` as the place for fixtures shared across multiple test modules
assertion_results.evidence: "Put the shared setup in a conftest.py at the root of your tests directory (tests/conftest.py) so every test module can use it automatically."

assertion_results.text: The answer mentions choosing an appropriate fixture scope such as function, module, or session
assertion_results.evidence: Mentions and demonstrates scopes: "session-scoped fixture for the database engine/schema creation", "function-scoped fixture for a transactional session", and in the example: @pytest.fixture(scope="module") for app.
